prompt_text:
Analyze the following python source code for violations of the Open/Closed Principle (OCP).

Use a TWO-STAGE reasoning process:

## Stage 1 — List design smells (silent, for your own reasoning)
Identify relevant low-level design smells you observe in the code:
- God Class, Feature Envy, Shotgun Surgery, Divergent Change
- Long Method, Large Class, Long Parameter List
- Refused Bequest, Inappropriate Intimacy, Message Chains
- Switch Statements on type, Data Clumps
Think through these internally....
raw_response:
{
  "findings": [
    {
      "file_path": "requests/src/requests/utils.py",
      "entity_name": "super_len",
      "entity_type": "function",
      "line_start": 135,
      "line_end": 207,
      "principle": "OCP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Uses type-checking and attribute inspection to determine length instead of polymorphism.",
      "reasoning": "The function uses a 'Switch Statement on type' smell, employing multiple hasattr and isinstance checks to determine how to calculate an object's length. Adding a new type of stream or object that requires a different length calculation logic necessitates modifying this function, violating the principle that the entity should be closed for modification."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "requests/src/requests/sessions.py",
      "entity_name": "SessionRedirectMixin.rebuild_method",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 312,
      "line_end": 332,
      "principle": "OCP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Hardcoded status code checks to determine HTTP method changes during redirects.",
      "reasoning": "This method contains hardcoded logic (elif-style checks) for specific HTTP status codes (301, 302, 303) to decide when to switch a request method to GET. Supporting new redirect behaviors or additional status codes requires modifying the existing method logic rather than extending it through a strategy or registry."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "requests/src/requests/adapters.py",
      "entity_name": "HTTPAdapter.proxy_manager_for",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 183,
      "line_end": 214,
      "principle": "OCP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Uses hardcoded scheme checks to select the appropriate proxy manager.",
      "reasoning": "The method uses hardcoded string matching (startswith('socks')) to determine which ProxyManager class to instantiate. To support a new proxy protocol (e.g., SSH, FTP), a developer must modify this method to add new conditional branches, which violates OCP."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "requests/src/requests/models.py",
      "entity_name": "PreparedRequest.prepare_body",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 500,
      "line_end": 561,
      "principle": "OCP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Uses complex conditional logic to handle different request body types.",
      "reasoning": "The method relies on internal type-checking and conditional logic to dispatch processing for 'json', 'files', 'data', and 'streams'. Adding a new body serialization format or a custom data handler requires modifying this central method, indicating a lack of a pluggable strategy for body preparation."
    }
  ]
}

findings.description: Uses type-checking and attribute inspection to determine length instead of polymorphism.
findings.reasoning: The function uses a 'Switch Statement on type' smell, employing multiple hasattr and isinstance checks to determine how to calculate an object's length. Adding a new type of stream or object that requires a different length calculation logic necessitates modifying this function, violating the principle that the entity should be closed for modification.

findings.description: Hardcoded status code checks to determine HTTP method changes during redirects.
findings.reasoning: This method contains hardcoded logic (elif-style checks) for specific HTTP status codes (301, 302, 303) to decide when to switch a request method to GET. Supporting new redirect behaviors or additional status codes requires modifying the existing method logic rather than extending it through a strategy or registry.

findings.description: Uses hardcoded scheme checks to select the appropriate proxy manager.
findings.reasoning: The method uses hardcoded string matching (startswith('socks')) to determine which ProxyManager class to instantiate. To support a new proxy protocol (e.g., SSH, FTP), a developer must modify this method to add new conditional branches, which violates OCP.

findings.description: Uses complex conditional logic to handle different request body types.
findings.reasoning: The method relies on internal type-checking and conditional logic to dispatch processing for 'json', 'files', 'data', and 'streams'. Adding a new body serialization format or a custom data handler requires modifying this central method, indicating a lack of a pluggable strategy for body preparation.
